t USE_LR_SCHEDULE:\\n        return None\\n    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 20\\n    warmup_steps = min(max(total_steps // 10, 1), 20)\\n\\n    def lr_lambda(step: int) -> float:\\n        if step < warmup_steps:\\n            return float(step + 1) / float(warmup_steps)\\n        progress = (step - warmup_steps) / max(1, total_steps - warmup_steps)\\n        cosine = 0.5 * (1.0 + np.cos(np.pi * progress))\\n        return 0.1 + 0.9 * cosine\\n\\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.LambdaLR(optimizer, lr_lambda=lr_lambda)\\n\",\"text\":\"\"}],\"primary_mode\":\"summaries\",\"rationale\":\"Use the fixed 256-step budget more effectively with a short warmup and cosine decay tied to the actual run length, paired with a higher peak learning rate for faster early progress.\",\"secondary_modes\":[\"topk\"]}"}}
